rules_applied.RULE8: FIRES — Draw 31.02% > 25% threshold. Moderate shrink on extreme attacking props.
rules_applied.RULE15: Mild consideration — Canada at 26.32% win probability, just above 15-20% threshold. Minimal RULE15 effect on David SOT (discount via SUI defensive quality instead).
rules_applied.FLOOR_0.25: Applied to Q9 (Xhaka SOT) — computed probability ~20%, floored at 0.25.
rules_applied.SURVIVAL_MATCH_CORNERS: Does NOT apply — neither team is eliminated. Both already qualified. No survival desperation.
rules_applied.BOTH_QUALIFIED_CONTEXT: SUI needs WIN to top group. CAN needs only DRAW to top on GD (+6 vs +3). Motivation asymmetry: SUI more aggressive, CAN slightly more conservative.
rules_applied.RULE_FOULS: Canada high-press style (19 fouls while attacking in GD2) vs SUI compact mid-block (7 fouls GD2). High-press team fouls more. RULE_FOULS direction: Canada more fouls.

gd3_implications.SUI_need: WIN to top group — stay in Vancouver (favorable for Canada fanbase away from home)
gd3_implications.CAN_need: DRAW sufficient to top group on goal difference. Win also tops group.
gd3_implications.group_winner_advantage: Winner gets favorable R32/R16 bracket position, likely plays in Vancouver for multiple rounds.

SUI_GD1_vs_Qatar.result: Draw — Qatar equalized via own goal from set piece (Muheim 90+1')
SUI_GD1_vs_Qatar.note: SUI led most of game, conceded to set piece in injury time

SUI_GD2_vs_Bosnia.result: WIN — ALL SUI goals came after 74'! 0-0 at HT. Bosnia red card Muharemovic 80'.
SUI_GD2_vs_Bosnia.note: Massive late burst driven by red card advantage — not reflective of open-play dominance

CAN_GD1_vs_Bosnia.note: David started but didn't score; Canada struggled to convert against BIH defense

CAN_GD2_vs_Qatar.result: WIN — Blowout. David hat trick (29', 45+3', 90+2'). Qatar had 2 red cards.
CAN_GD2_vs_Qatar.note: David: 8 shots, 5 SOT. Entirely blowout context — Qatar went to 9 men.
CAN_GD2_vs_Qatar.koné_out: Koné OUT (broken leg) — confirmed absent for SUI-CAN

Granit_Xhaka.club_2025_26: Sunderland FC (Premier League) — moved from Leverkusen July 2025
Granit_Xhaka.gd2_sot_context: Converted penalty at 90+7' — the only source of his SOT in GD2
Granit_Xhaka.note: Structurally a passer, not a shooter. Penalty is his only reliable SOT route.

Jonathan_David.club_2025_26: Juventus (Serie A) — free transfer from Lille July 2025
Jonathan_David.gd2_context: Hat trick vs Qatar (who had 0 SOT, 2 red cards) — extreme blowout
Jonathan_David.note: World-class striker. Swiss defense (Akanji, Elvedi) will limit him vs Qatar pace — discounted to lambda=1.5

referee.name: Ramon Abatti Abel
referee.note: High-card disciplinarian. Career rate strongly supports 2+ cards in 2H.

market_vs_model_gap.BTTS_yes: market 58% vs model 44.81% — 13pp gap (market prices recent form higher)

Q1.question: Will Canada commit more fouls than Switzerland?
key_drivers: Canada GD2 vs Qatar: 19 fouls while attacking (high-press style inherent to Marsch 4-4-2)
Switzerland GD2 vs Bosnia: only 7 fouls (compact, disciplined mid-block)
In competitive match: Canada presses, SUI absorbs → CAN fouls more in pressing
Lambda model: P(CAN>SUI | λ_can=13, λ_sui=10) = 69.9%
Q1.caveat: If SUI leads and CAN chases, trailing team fouls more. But opening 60min favors Canada.

Q2.question: Will Switzerland have 2 or more offsides?
key_drivers: Canada high defensive line (high-press = high line = offside trap opportunities)
SUI fast wingers (Ndoye, Vargas, Okafor/Manzambi) run in behind on transitions
Lambda 1.3-1.5 estimated: P(≥2) = 34-44%
No GD-specific SUI offside count available

Q3.question: Will there be 2 or more total cards in the second half?
key_drivers: Abatti career 4.85 YC/game → expected 2H cards ~2.4 → P(≥2) = 64-70%
Physical, pressing match between two athletic teams
Both teams already qualified: players SLIGHTLY more cautious about suspension — modest shade down
Late-game desperation (SUI chasing a win if drawing) = late yellow cards

Q4.question: Will both teams score AND the match have 3 or more total goals?
key_drivers: Model: P(BTTS AND ≥3) = 31.54%
RULE8 FIRES: draw 31.02% → cagey, tactical match → fewer goals shade
Market-implied joint probability: ~27-31% (BTTS 58% × cond. on ≥3 = ~53% → joint ~0.307)
GD1 matches were 1-1 (SUI-QAT and CAN-BIH) — low-scoring competitive contests

Q5.question: Will a penalty kick be awarded?
key_drivers: Abatti career ~0.32 pens/game
Historical overpricing pattern on penalty questions — shade below theoretical rate
Season 2025-26: 0 penalties in 2 matches (small sample but directional)

Q6.question: Will Switzerland have more 2H shots on target than Canada?
key_drivers: Model: P(SUI more 2H SOT) = 44.97% — essentially coin-flip
RULE8 compresses toward 50%: 44.97% → ~48%
Switzerland technically superior; Canada GD1 only 4 SOT total
CAN 10 SOT in GD2 was vs Qatar 9 men — inflated

Q7.question: Will Switzerland win the match?
key_drivers: Model: 42.65%; Market: ~40%; Average: ~41%
Motivation asymmetry: SUI MUST WIN to top group; CAN happy with draw
+3pp for motivation: SUI will push harder, CAN may sit deeper in 2H with lead or draw
RULE8 does not suppress WIN props (only extreme attacking props)

Q8.question: Will the second half have 2 or more total goals?
key_drivers: Model: P(2H ≥ 2 goals) = 30.62%
RULE8: tactical match = fewer 2H goals
SUI GD1: 0 SUI goals in 2H vs Qatar (held 1-0 until conceded in 90+)
CAN GD1: only 1 2H goal vs Bosnia (in 83')

Q9.question: Will Granit Xhaka have at least 1 shot on target?
key_drivers: 0.09 SOT/90 career rate at Leverkusen (3 SOT in 33 games) — structural shooting limitation
Computed probability (open play + penalty path): ~20%
FLOOR_0.25 applied per standing rule
GD2 SOT was a converted penalty — unlikely without another penalty opportunity
FanDuel scorer +1600 confirms market knows he rarely scores/shoots
MAJOR EDGE: crowd likely ~0.40; our floor is 0.25 → ~15pp NO edge

Q10.question: Will Jonathan David have at least 1 shot on target?
key_drivers: Historical Lille SOT/90: 2.0-2.5 → P(≥1) = 86-92% in normal match
GD2: 5 SOT from 8 shots (hat trick) vs Qatar — discount for Switzerland quality defense
Discount to lambda=1.5 vs SUI (Akanji, Elvedi, Rodriguez) → P(≥1) = 77.7%
Mild RULE15 discount for CAN 26.32% underdog status: -7pp → 70%
FanDuel scorer +225 implies ~31% scoring probability → SOT much higher
Edge: crowd ~0.60; our estimate 0.70 → ~10pp YES edge

edge_ranking.note: Xhaka floor. Structural 0.09 SOT/90. Crowd ~0.40. Biggest edge of match.
Canada high-press fouls. GD2 evidence: CAN 19 vs SUI 7.
SUI motivation asymmetry. Model+market blend ~41%, +3pp motivation.
Low 2H goals. RULE8 + model. Crowd may be ~0.38.
Abatti high card rate. Physical match.
SUI more 2H SOT. Essentially no edge.

questions.question: Will Canada commit more fouls than Switzerland?
Will Switzerland be caught offside 2 or more times?
Will there be 2 or more total cards shown in the second half?
Will both teams score AND the match have 3 or more total goals?
Will a penalty kick be awarded in the match?
Will Switzerland have more shots on target than Canada in the second half?
Will the second half have 2 or more total goals?
Will Granit Xhaka have at least 1 shot on target?
Will Jonathan David have at least 1 shot on target?

key_learnings: BTTS+3+ goals (Q4) = YES in a 2-1 match — our 0.30 was over-suppressed by RULE8. Both teams scored AND total was exactly 3. RULE8 pushed us too far from model's 0.315.
Q8 (2H 2+ goals) = YES — our 0.30 missed again. At least 2 second-half goals in a close 2-1 match.
Q1 (CAN more fouls) = NO — Switzerland fouled more despite being expected to be the less press-intensive team. In a 2-1 match, the dynamics of who chases and who presses shifted prediction.

Jonathan David SOT (Q10) = NO — our 0.70 overestimated. Swiss defense (Akanji, Elvedi) suppressed David effectively. His GD2 hat-trick vs 9-man Qatar did not transfer. Should have been closer to 0.55.
Xhaka SOT floor (Q9, 0.25) confirmed correct — structural DM prior held, outcome NO, +13.40 RBP our best result.
Q3 (2H 2+ cards) = NO — Abatti's high card rate didn't materialize in a 2-1 result. Cards often cluster in desperate situations; 2-1 may have been less frantic than expected.
